questions.question: Implement an Ethernet LAN using 5 nodes and set up 3 traffic nodes. Calculate the congestion window for different source-destination pairs. Assume a maximum bandwidth of 100 Mbps and a round-trip time (RTT) of 2 ms. Plot the congestion window for each pair and explain the factors that affect it.
questions.hint: Use the formulas for congestion window calculation and consider factors like bandwidth, latency, and packet loss.

questions.question: A traffic node in an Ethernet LAN with 8 nodes experiences high packet loss due to interference. Calculate the congestion window and bandwidth-delay product for this node. Assuming a maximum bandwidth of 200 Mbps and a round-trip time (RTT) of 2 ms, determine the minimum bandwidth required to support reliable data transfer.
questions.hint: Use the formulas for congestion window and bandwidth-delay product, and consider the effects of packet loss on data transfer.

questions.question: An Ethernet LAN with 10 nodes is configured to use a congestion control algorithm that adjusts the congestion window based on the number of nodes and the maximum bandwidth. If the number of nodes increases by 20% and the maximum bandwidth decreases by 30%, how will this affect the congestion window for each node? Assume the initial congestion window is 400 kbps.
questions.hint: Use the formulas for congestion window calculation and consider the combined effects of increased nodes and decreased bandwidth.
